macro_summary: Monday's session was marked by a temporary halt in US-Iran hostilities after a weekend of renewed strikes, as both sides agreed to cease attacks ahead of further talks (per Reuters). WTI recovered 0.2% to $70.17 after briefly dipping below $70 on Friday, though oil remains more than 20% below its pre-conflict peak. U.S. equities were mixed: the S&P 500 slipped 0.5% as AI-related semiconductor anxiety (SOXX -5.0%) weighed, while bond yields edged lower (US10Y 4.37%) and the VIX eased to 18.4. China's widening export controls on Japanese entities added to geopolitical friction.
commodities_note: WTI held around $70 as the US-Iran ceasefire held and Hormuz traffic resumed, but traders remain cautious per Reuters commentary on the chaotic rebalancing. Gold at $4,078 was nearly flat, as safe-haven demand eased alongside the VIX.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain extended on technicals (all three >5% above 50-day) as US10Y stubbornly above 4.20% and MAS S$NEER appreciation persists. Divergence: OCBC and DBS show stronger price momentum than UOB, but NIM compression from dual channels is uniform; no bank is presenting a clean entry yet. Reversal catalyst: a durable break below 4.20% US10Y and a MAS slope shift in October.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally cautious — awaiting NIM relief triggers.
singapore.key_development: No change to the dual-channel NIM headwind; US10Y held above 4.2% and next MAS decision not until October 2026.

semiconductors.summary: Semis cluster is under pressure from AI capex anxiety, with SOXX -5.03% and nearly all names extending or breaking below 50-day. NVDA and TSM are rare bright spots showing oversold rebounds; MU and SK Hynix remain extreme-extended on HBM demand. Internal divergence: memory stocks defend gains while compute/ASML slip. The cluster's thesis status is 'Structurally bullish, tactically wounded' — AI demand is real, but valuations and extension require disciplined entry.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically wounded — wait for pullbacks.
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX dropped 5.0% on AI capex sustainability fears, dragging most semis lower despite Micron's record earnings.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverges sharply: GOOG and MSFT offer oversold entry setups near multi-month lows, while AMZN and PLTR are deep in caution territory. The cluster's AI capex narrative is under severe scrutiny, but Google's TPU ramp and MSFT's Azure ex-OpenAI RPO provide cleaner demand signals. Thesis_status: 'Mixed — GOOG/MSFT near entry, AMZN/PLTR in penalty box.'
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — GOOG/MSFT near entry, AMZN/PLTR in penalty box.
big_tech.key_development: GOOG regained footing on strong TPU demand and cloud growth, while MSFT recovered 4.9% on value buying despite lingering AI spending concerns.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR corrects 15-20% from recent highs on optical-interconnect profit-taking, as NVDA's multi-year capacity commitments underpin demand.

interconnected.name: Cadence Design Systems
interconnected.reason: EDA leader with expanded DTCO collaboration on Intel 14A; indirect read-through to ASML/INTC decoupling and US export controls.
interconnected.entry_note: Add if CDNS pulls back 10% from current levels after SOXX selloff, as EDA demand is sticky and 14A ramp requires long-term tool licenses.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Data center switching leader; benefits directly from AI bandwidth growth tied to NVDA/MU infrastructure buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add if ANET retests its 200-day moving average on AI spending jitters, as hyperscaler networks are irreversible multi-year projects.

active_risks: US-Iran ceasefire holds but fragile; renewed tit-for-tat strikes flared over weekend before a halt to attacks was agreed (per Reuters, Jun 29). Hormuz traffic resuming but chaotic rebalancing keeps oil risk premium partially intact.
China widens export controls on Japan, blacklisting 40 entities including defense-linked firms (per CNBC, Jun 29), escalating tech decoupling in Asia.
US AI export restrictions: Anthropic's Mythos 5 cleared for vetted US organizations, but Fable 5 suspended (per Forbes, Jun 28), deepening bifurcation in advanced AI access.
Fed hawkish hold reinforced by 4.1% PCE; rate hike expectations persist, pressuring growth and technology multiples.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil near pre-conflict levels temporarily reduces energy cost fears for AI datacenters, but shipowner caution per Reuters keeps supply-risk premium partially alive; ASML and INTC face export-control headwinds amid US-China chip decoupling. Tech rout (SOXX -5.0%) is partly fueled by rate-hike fears and AI spending sustainability doubts.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US and Iran agreed to halt attacks after weekend strikes (per Reuters); China widened export curbs to 40 Japanese entities (per CNBC); Anthropic's Mythos 5 cleared for US use (per Forbes).

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, WTI sticks near $70-75; peace talks progress slowly. Fed holds through Q3, rate hikes remain on table, but oil drop limits inflation upside. Semis and BigTech grind sideways; D05_SI, O39_SI stay extended, no entry; NVDA and GOOG provide oversold bounces but broad extension persists.

optimistic.description: US-Iran peace deal reached, oil drops to $60, inflation expectations cool sharply. Fed signals pause, US10Y falls below 4.0%. Semis (NVDA, AMD, MU) rally, Singapore banks benefit from NIM relief; entry triggers fire on TSM and MSFT.

pessimistic.description: Conflict re-escalates, oil spikes above $90; stagflation fears return, VIX >25. Tech selloff deepens, semis cluster breaks below 200-day averages; NVDA and GOOG entries invalidated; all positions on hold.

wildcard.description: China unexpectedly eases rare-earth and chip export curbs as part of a trade deal, sparking a broad AI capex surge. ASML, LITE, and INTC rally on renewed foundry optimism; semi stocks re-rate sharply higher.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, selective entry on oversold leaders like NVDA and GOOG, avoid energy plays. Wait for clearer NFP and Fed signals before adding positions in TSM and MSFT.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously selective — oversold AI leaders emerging, but broad extension persists.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
